scope: Renderer-neutral player settings and accessibility metadata with a thin Unity authoring/application adapter

sources.license_or_terms: Microsoft Learn terms; facts and coverage vocabulary only
sources.maintenance_evidence: Current Xbox Accessibility Guidelines index reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Game accessibility coverage and non-compliance boundary
admitted_claims: Game accessibility settings span text, contrast, multimodal cues, captions, audio, input, difficulty, navigation, motion, photosensitivity and discoverability
Guidelines are design, development and test guidance rather than legal-compliance certification
A game should scope applicable barriers instead of assuming every guideline applies identically
excluded_uses: No checklist item becomes a mandatory Core setting
No metadata tag proves implementation, usability, certification or legal compliance

admitted_claims: Player choice across input mechanisms reduces barriers created by fixed physical-control assumptions
Settings semantics should refer to player intent and policy rather than one hardware control
excluded_uses: The Settings family does not implement rebinding or device layouts
Input guidance does not prove support for a controller, hand, gaze, voice or switch device

sources.maintenance_evidence: Current XAG 117 and version history reviewed 2026-07-16
admitted_claims: Motion-related settings can expose control over camera shake, camera bob, field of view, sensitivity and moving or auto-updating content
Relevant choices should be discoverable without requiring gameplay exposure first
excluded_uses: No medical outcome, XR comfort threshold or device behavior is inferred
The Core does not implement camera or locomotion effects

admitted_claims: Accessibility features need discoverable player-facing descriptions
A platform feature tag has criteria and validation beyond merely exposing a similarly named option
excluded_uses: XR Foundry metadata is not an Xbox or Accessible Games Initiative store tag
No platform listing claim is generated automatically

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; facts only, no copied implementation
admitted_claims: ScriptableObjects are project data containers independent of GameObject instances
Shared authored definitions and presets can be referenced without duplicating configuration
excluded_uses: Authored assets are not mutable player-setting storage
ScriptableObject authoring does not prescribe a UI renderer or concrete game policy

sources.license_or_terms: Epic Developer Community documentation terms; architecture facts only, no copied implementation
sources.maintenance_evidence: Current Unreal Engine 5.7 Lyra Game Settings documentation reviewed 2026-07-16
sources.admitted_role: Professional settings registry, identity, data-source and presentation separation
admitted_claims: A shipped sample uses a settings registry with unique developer-facing identities, typed setting variants, defaults, descriptions, edit dependencies and conditions
Runtime getters/setters and data sources are separate from visual presentation rules
Platform traits and contextual conditions can hide, disable or explain unavailable settings without hard-coding one platform into setting identity
excluded_uses: No Unreal source code, macro system, UMG renderer, dynamic reflection path or class hierarchy is copied
The documentation does not prove XR Foundry transaction, rollback, persistence or Unity behavior

sources.maintenance_evidence: 1,985 GitHub stars at review; active on 2026-07-16; published releases and tests; framework used by the shipped osu! game
sources.admitted_role: Strongly adopted evidence for typed configuration, defaults, ranges, change notification and replaceable storage
admitted_claims: Typed configuration entries can carry defaults and numeric bounds while exposing old/new value change events
Configuration lookup, typed values and storage implementation can be separated
Configuration logging needs an explicit boundary for private values
excluded_uses: No source code or API shape is copied
Its enum lookup, bindable graph and INI provider are not imposed on consumers
It is not admitted as evidence for transactional multi-setting rollback

sources.license_or_terms: Apple Developer documentation terms; facts only
sources.admitted_role: Immersive motion choice and non-universal comfort boundary
admitted_claims: Motion should be purposeful, optional where possible, and not the only communication channel
visionOS guidance treats peripheral, full-field, world-rotation and sustained motion as platform-specific comfort considerations
excluded_uses: Apple guidance is not universal device support or a Unity implementation prescription
No numeric comfort threshold, medical claim or named-headset result is transferred

context_only_not_derivation_inputs.reason: Useful cross-check for barrier vocabulary, but Microsoft first-party guidance is the load-bearing derivation source

source_gate_non_claims: No source grants package maturity, release, certification, compliance, medical, platform or device authority
The first implementation is a reusable semantic and authoring substrate, not a complete product settings experience
Popularity or official ownership never substitutes for exact repository and consumer verification
